evals.prompt: How do I find outdated direct dependencies of my project root with vlt?
evals.expected_output: A vlt query using ':root > :outdated' (optionally with a specifier like major), with a one-line explanation. Concise — query first, no long preamble.
assertions: proposes-root-outdated-selector: at least one extracted selector matches /:root\s*>\s*:outdated/
all-selectors-parse: every `vlt query '<sel>'` selector in the answer parses via @vltpkg/dss-parser
selector-executes-on-fixture-graph: the primary (structural) selector runs through Query.search() against the in-memory fixture graph without throwing
query-first-style: a fenced code block containing the vlt query appears before any multi-sentence explanation

evals.prompt: Write a DSS query for prod dependencies of my workspaces that have a MIT license.
evals.expected_output: A vlt query like ':workspace > :prod[license=MIT]' — workspace anchor, direct-dep combinator, prod filter chained with the [license=MIT] attribute. Must NOT suggest ':license(mit)': the :license() pseudo only accepts category kinds (copyleft, unlicensed, none, ...), so specific license IDs require the attribute form.
assertions: uses-license-attribute-form: a selector matches /\[license\^?=\s*['"]?MIT/i (attribute form for a specific license ID)
avoids-invalid-license-pseudo: the answer does NOT propose :license(mit) — :license() only accepts category kinds, so this would throw 'Expected a valid license kind'
workspace-prod-anchored: a selector matches /:workspace\s*>\s*:prod/
all-selectors-parse: every extracted selector parses via @vltpkg/dss-parser

evals.prompt: What vlt query shows me which packages directly depend on react? I keep writing '#react > *' and getting the wrong thing.
evals.expected_output: Inverted-direction query using ':has(> #react)' (dependents), correcting the user's '#react > *' (which selects react's dependencies, not its dependents).
assertions: uses-has-inversion: a selector matches /:has\(\s*>\s*(#react|\[name=react\])\s*\)/
corrects-direction-mistake: states that '#react > *' selects react's DEPENDENCIES (children), i.e. wrong direction for finding dependents (grader quotes evidence)
does-not-present-wrong-selector-as-answer: '#react > *' is not offered as the solution
all-selectors-parse: every extracted selector parses via @vltpkg/dss-parser

evals.prompt: What does the vlt query ':workspace > :prod:cve(*)' do?
evals.expected_output: Left-to-right decomposition: workspaces anchor, direct dependencies, production type, has any CVE. Should mention security selectors hit the network (Socket data).
assertions: covers-workspace-anchor: explanation mentions workspaces as the starting set (/workspace/i)
covers-direct-prod-segment: explanation mentions direct AND production dependencies (/direct/i and /prod/i)
covers-cve-segment: explanation says it matches packages with any known CVE (/cve/i)
mentions-security-data-source: notes that :cve() uses Socket security data / a network call (/socket|network/i)

evals.prompt: We just got a supply-chain security alert at work. What vlt queries should I run to audit our dependency graph for malware, typosquats, and obfuscated code?
evals.expected_output: A supply-chain sweep using threat selectors — e.g. ':malware(critical), :squat, :obfuscated' (union or separate queries). Notes Socket data / network latency. Offers scoping or follow-up queries.
assertions: uses-threat-selectors: selectors include :malware AND at least one of :squat / :obfuscated / :suspicious
all-selectors-parse: every extracted selector parses via @vltpkg/dss-parser
mentions-security-data-source: notes Socket data / network fetch (/socket|network/i)
offers-related-queries: at least 2 distinct selectors offered

evals.prompt: Which of my dev dependencies can run shell commands or hit the network? We're locking down CI.
evals.expected_output: Capability query composing :dev with :shell and :network — e.g. ':dev:shell, :dev:network' (chained AND, comma OR). Explains capabilities = what the code can do (Socket static analysis).
assertions: uses-capability-selectors: a selector chains :dev with :shell or :network (e.g. :dev:shell)
covers-both-capabilities: both shell and network are queried (union or two queries)
all-selectors-parse: every extracted selector parses via @vltpkg/dss-parser
explains-capability-meaning: explains these match what code CAN do / Socket analysis (/socket|static|capab|can /i)

evals.prompt: Before our release I want to check whether any prod deps of our workspaces have high or critical severity vulnerabilities. What's the vlt query?
evals.expected_output: Severity-filtered composition anchored at workspaces — e.g. ':workspace > :prod:severity(">=high")' (or union of severity(high), severity(critical)). Explains the comparator and mentions Socket/network.
assertions: uses-severity-filter: a selector uses :severity(...) or :sev(...)
anchors-workspace-prod: a selector matches /:workspace\s*>\s*:prod/
all-selectors-parse: every extracted selector parses via @vltpkg/dss-parser
mentions-security-data-source: notes Socket data / network fetch (/socket|network/i)
